color_profiles.name: Raspberry / Crystal Rhodochrosite
color_profiles.cause: High Mn content with minimal Fe/Ca dilution; Mn2+ d-d transition.

color_profiles.name: Inca Rose (Banded Stalactitic)
color_profiles.cause: Alternating layers with varying Mn/Ca/Fe ratios; banding reflects changing fluid chemistry.

color_profiles.name: Orange-Brown Rhodochrosite
color_profiles.cause: Higher Fe substitution (toward siderite) diluting and warming the pink.

visual_notes: The banded 'Inca Rose' stalactitic form from Argentina — polished to reveal concentric pink-and-white rings — is one of the most recognized ornamental stone patterns in the gem world. Crystallized raspberry-red specimens from Colorado are among the most sought-after collector mineral specimens globally.

optical_effects.fluorescence: Weak; some specimens show pale pink or orange fluorescence under UV
